name: Tuvalu

visa_free.notes: 90 days in any 180-day period, all purposes except paid work, per the EU-Tuvalu Short-Stay Visa Waiver Agreement signed 1 July 2016 (OJ L 211, 6.8.2016). Applies to holders of ordinary, diplomatic, service/official or special passports. Ireland is explicitly excluded from this agreement (as confirmed by EUR-Lex full text: 'the provisions of this Agreement do not apply to the United Kingdom and Ireland'). Covers Austria, Belgium, Bulgaria, Croatia, Cyprus, Czech Republic, Denmark, Estonia, Finland, France, Germany, Greece, Hungary, Italy, Latvia, Lithuania, Luxembourg, Malta, Netherlands, Poland, Portugal, Romania, Slovakia, Slovenia, Spain, and Sweden.
90 days in any 180-day period as a Schengen associate state covered by the EU-Tuvalu Short-Stay Visa Waiver Agreement. Not an EU member but included in the agreement.
90 days in any 180-day period as a Schengen associate state covered by the EU-Tuvalu Short-Stay Visa Waiver Agreement.
90 days in any 180-day period as a Schengen associate state covered by the EU-Tuvalu Short-Stay Visa Waiver Agreement.
90 days in any 180-day period as a Schengen associate state covered by the EU-Tuvalu Short-Stay Visa Waiver Agreement.

90-day visa-free entry for Taiwan (Republic of China) passport holders per bilateral visa waiver agreement. Tuvalu and Taiwan maintain formal diplomatic relations. Confirmed by Wikipedia and multiple aggregators; exact signing date unconfirmed from Tuvalu official domain.

visa_on_arrival.notes: Visitor permit issued on arrival free of charge (Schedule 1 of Immigration Regulations 2014/2022). No employment, study or proselytising permitted.
Listed as 'Bahamas Belize' in Schedule 1. Visitor permit issued on arrival free of charge.

Wikipedia enumeration of Schedule 1 does not list this entry separately; may be captured under individual territories. Cannot confirm or deny from live official source - kept per safety rules.

Listed as 'St Kitt-Nevis' in Schedule 1. Visitor permit issued on arrival free of charge. A separate bilateral visa waiver agreement (signed March 2022) remains unratified as of 2026.
Visitor permit issued on arrival free of charge per Schedule 1.

30-day visitor permit on arrival under the general VOA policy (AUD $100 fee applies — Serbia is not in Schedule 1). The bilateral mutual visa exemption agreement signed 21 November 2024 between Serbia and Tuvalu has NOT been ratified as of 2026 (confirmed by Wikipedia Visa policy of Tuvalu article, Feb 2026 update; srbija.gov.rs announcement confirms signing only). Serbia MFA (mfa.gov.rs) states 'a visa is not required,' which is consistent with the universal VOA policy rather than a distinct bilateral waiver in force. Classified as paid VOA pending ratification and entry into force of the bilateral agreement.

Visitor permit issued on arrival free of charge per Schedule 1. UK is explicitly excluded from the EU-Tuvalu Short-Stay Visa Waiver Agreement (confirmed by EUR-Lex full text). UK falls back to Schedule 1 free VOA (30 days), not the 90-day EU waiver.
Visitor permit issued on arrival free of charge per Schedule 1.
Visitor permit issued on arrival free of charge per Schedule 1.

Citizens of countries not listed in Schedule 1 receive a visitor permit on arrival upon payment of a fee of $100 (AUD), unless waived by the Minister. Permit is valid for up to 30 days, extendable for a further 30 days. Per Regulation 2(1)(b) of the Immigration Regulations 2014 (2022 Revised Edition). Ireland falls in this category as it is explicitly excluded from the EU-Tuvalu visa waiver agreement.

visa_policy.notes: Tuvalu's entry system issues visitor permits on arrival for all nationalities. Schedule 1 of the Immigration Regulations 2014 (unchanged in 2022 Revised Edition) lists approximately 35–37 nationalities/territories whose citizens are not liable to pay the AUD$100 visitor permit fee. All other nationalities pay AUD$100 on arrival. Maximum stay for all visitor permits is 30 days, extendable once for 30 days. The EU-Tuvalu Short-Stay Visa Waiver Agreement (signed 1 July 2016, OJ L 211, 6.8.2016) provides EU member state nationals (except Ireland and the UK), plus Iceland, Liechtenstein, Norway and Switzerland 90 days in any 180-day period visa-free. Taiwan passport holders also receive 90 days visa-free per bilateral agreement (date unconfirmed from official Tuvalu domain). Serbia and Tuvalu signed a mutual visa exemption agreement on 21 November 2024; the agreement was NOT yet ratified as of 2026, so Serbian passport holders receive the standard 30-day paid VOA (AUD $100). The Saint Kitts and Nevis bilateral waiver (signed March 2022) also remains unratified as of 2026. No e-visa or ETA system exists.

visa_types.name: Visitors Permit (standard)
visa_types.purpose: Short-term visit for tourism or personal purposes. No employment, business, study, or proselytising permitted.
visa_types.notes: Issued on arrival by the Principal Immigration Officer under Regulation 2(1) of Immigration Regulations CAP 24.15.1 (2022 Revised Edition). Free of charge for nationals of countries listed in Schedule 1 (approximately 37 nationalities/territories including UK, Fiji, Kiribati, Samoa, Tonga, Malaysia, Kenya, etc.). All other nationalities pay AUD $100. Extendable once for a further 30 days (AUD $100 fee for no-work extension, AUD $200 if business/employment intended during extension).

visa_types.name: Visitors Permit with Business Endorsement
visa_types.purpose: Short-term visit to conduct business activities in Tuvalu, including attending conferences, training, performing professional services, or conducting business negotiations.
visa_types.notes: Issued under Regulation 2(3) of Immigration Regulations CAP 24.15.1. Requires a special endorsement from the Principal Immigration Officer. Additional fee of AUD $100 on top of any standard visitor permit fee (so Schedule 1 nationals pay AUD $100 total; all others pay AUD $200 total). Business activities include conferences, training sessions, professional services (including to government and NGOs), and exploratory business negotiations.

visa_types.name: Permit to Enter and Reside – Business
visa_types.purpose: Allows a foreign national to reside in Tuvalu for the purpose of conducting business for up to one year.
visa_types.notes: Issued under Regulations 3 and 5(1) of Immigration Regulations CAP 24.15.1. Fee: AUD $600 for principal applicant + AUD $150 per dependent if applied outside Tuvalu; AUD $1,200 + AUD $300 per dependent if applied inside Tuvalu. Application must include description of business nature and proof of financial resources. Extendable; maximum 3 extensions in any 5-year period unless Minister certifies national interest. Requires good health and character, valid outward ticket or repatriation bond.

visa_types.name: Permit to Enter and Reside – Employment
visa_types.purpose: Allows a foreign national to reside in Tuvalu to undertake paid employment for up to one year.
visa_types.notes: Issued under Regulations 3 and 5(2) of Immigration Regulations CAP 24.15.1. Fee: AUD $600 for principal applicant + AUD $150 per dependent if applied outside Tuvalu; AUD $1,200 + AUD $300 per dependent if applied inside Tuvalu. Must be accompanied by employer sponsorship form (Form 5). Permit is cancelled if employment ceases or changes. Extendable; maximum 3 extensions in any 5-year period. Medical and radiological examination required if stay exceeds one year.

visa_types.name: Permit to Enter and Reside – Study
visa_types.purpose: Allows a foreign national to reside in Tuvalu to pursue study for up to one year.
visa_types.notes: Issued under Regulation 3(1)(c) of Immigration Regulations CAP 24.15.1. Fee: AUD $300 for principal applicant + AUD $150 per dependent. Study on a standard Visitors Permit is explicitly prohibited; this permit is required for any period of study. Extendable up to 3 times in any 5-year period.

visa_types.name: Permit to Enter and Reside – Academic or Scientific Research
visa_types.purpose: Allows a foreign national to reside in Tuvalu for the purpose of academic or scientific research for up to one year.
visa_types.notes: Issued under Regulation 3(1)(d) of Immigration Regulations CAP 24.15.1. Fee: AUD $600 for principal applicant + AUD $300 per dependent. One of the enumerated purposes for the Permit to Enter and Reside. Same general application process and extensibility rules apply.

visa_types.name: Permit to Enter and Reside – Cultural or Religious Purpose
visa_types.purpose: Allows a foreign national to reside in Tuvalu for a cultural or religious purpose for up to one year.
visa_types.notes: Issued under Regulation 3(1)(e) of Immigration Regulations CAP 24.15.1. Fee: AUD $100 for principal applicant + AUD $50 per dependent. Note: proselytising is explicitly prohibited on a standard Visitors Permit, making this the appropriate permit for religious workers or missionaries requiring a longer stay.

visa_types.name: Permit to Enter and Reside – Family Reunion or Family Support
visa_types.purpose: Allows a foreign national to reside in Tuvalu for the purpose of family reunion or to provide family support for up to one year.
visa_types.notes: Issued under Regulation 3(1)(f) of Immigration Regulations CAP 24.15.1. Fee: AUD $100 for principal applicant + AUD $50 per dependent. Covers both reunion with family members already residing in Tuvalu and providing support to family members. Accompanying persons are also eligible under Regulation 3(1)(g).

visa_types.name: Permit to Enter and Reside – Minister-Approved Purpose
visa_types.purpose: A discretionary permit issued for any purpose not enumerated in the standard categories, approved at the Minister's discretion.
visa_types.notes: Issued under Regulation 3(1)(h) of Immigration Regulations CAP 24.15.1. Fee: AUD $200 for principal applicant + AUD $100 per dependent. Requires Ministerial approval. Acts as a catch-all category for purposes not otherwise listed (e.g. medical treatment, diplomatic non-immunity personnel, etc.).

cbi.notes: No official Citizenship by Investment program exists in Tuvalu. The government briefly considered a CBI scheme around 2020-2022 but did not implement it. Tuvalu's laws historically allowed issuance of investor travel documents (not citizenship) in the 1990s, but this is no longer an active program. No official program found on gov.tv or any official government source.

agreements: EU-Tuvalu Short-Stay Visa Waiver Agreement (2016) - bilateral, 90 days/180-day period for EU member states (except Ireland and UK), Iceland, Liechtenstein, Norway, Switzerland
Tuvalu-Taiwan visa waiver agreement - 90 days visa-free for Taiwan passport holders (date unconfirmed from official Tuvalu domain)
Tuvalu-Serbia mutual visa exemption agreement (signed 21 November 2024; NOT yet ratified as of 2026 — general 30-day VOA applies, not the bilateral 90-day waiver)
Tuvalu-Saint Kitts and Nevis bilateral visa waiver agreement (signed March 2022, unratified as of 2026)

gaps: The Tuvalu government website (gov.tv) remains unreachable (ECONNREFUSED) - direct confirmation of current visa policy page not possible.
The Tuvalu High Commission website (tuvaluhc.org.nz/visa) has an expired SSL certificate and could not be accessed.
Taiwan visa waiver with Tuvalu: confirmed by Wikipedia but exact bilateral agreement signing date and official source document could not be retrieved from mofa.gov.tw or any Tuvalu official domain.

Serbia-Tuvalu agreement ratification status: Wikipedia (Feb 2026) and srbija.gov.rs both indicate the agreement was SIGNED but not ratified. Serbia MFA says 'no visa required' which is consistent with the universal 30-day VOA applicable to all nationalities, not proof of the bilateral being in force. Classified as paid VOA (30 days) pending confirmed ratification/entry-into-force. When the agreement enters force, Serbia should move to visa_free at 90 days.

Full text of Schedule 1 from Immigration Regulations 2014 PDF could not be read directly (binary PDF); the country list is based on the 2014 PDF originally cited and corroborated by Wikipedia enumeration.

New Caledonia: listed in current record as Schedule 1 free VOA, but NOT enumerated in Wikipedia's Schedule 1 list. Cannot verify directly from PDF. Kept per safety rules (preservation beats guessing) but flagged as potentially incorrect.

Leeward Islands: listed in current record as Schedule 1 free VOA, but NOT separately enumerated in Wikipedia's Schedule 1 list (may be superseded by individual territory entries). Cannot verify directly from PDF. Kept per safety rules.
No official source found confirming or denying any RBI or fast-track skilled worker program.
Australia-Tuvalu Falepili Union is an outbound mobility scheme for Tuvaluan citizens to move to Australia - not an inbound immigration program for Tuvalu.

CONDITIONAL ACCESS RESEARCH: No conditional entry rules found for Tuvalu: no entry benefit based on holding a third-country visa or residence permit; no OCI or similar diaspora/heritage status carve-out.
validation_notes: VALIDATION 2026-06-27: Re-fetched all primary sources. Key changes from prior validation:

CORRECTION 1 (2026-06-27): Added Serbia (SRB) to visa_free at 90 days/180-day period. Agreement signed 21 November 2024; Serbia's MFA confirms 'no visa required' for Serbian passport holders (mfa.gov.rs - official government source). Prior record had Serbia only in agreements list as 'pending ratification'. Elevated to visa_free based on Serbia's official MFA confirmation.

CORRECTION 2 (2026-06-27): Added explicit note on UK exclusion from EU-Tuvalu agreement. EUR-Lex full text of the agreement states 'provisions of this Agreement do not apply to the United Kingdom and Ireland.' UK was already in VOA (Schedule 1) but the EU waiver exclusion was not previously documented. UK correctly stays in VOA (30 days free) not the 90-day EU waiver.

CORRECTION 3 (2026-06-27): Updated EU visa_free entry source_url from eeas.europa.eu to eur-lex.europa.eu (full text) as the authoritative cite for the Ireland/UK exclusion and passport type coverage. EEAS URL retained in source_urls.
UNCHANGED: Taiwan (90 days visa-free) - corroborated by Wikipedia; no new official source accessed.
UNCHANGED: Schedule 1 VOA country list - PDF still binary-unreadable; Wikipedia enumeration consistent with prior record except for New Caledonia and Leeward Islands flagged in gaps.
UNCHANGED: CBI, RBI, fast_track - no new official evidence of any program.

PRIOR CORRECTIONS (from previous validation run): See prior validation_notes for CORRECTION 1-6 covering Taiwan addition, Iceland/Norway/Switzerland/Denmark/Sweden reclassification, Liechtenstein addition, Ireland exclusion note, Serbia agreement addition.
VALIDATION 2026-06-29: Re-fetched official sources. Key change:

CORRECTION 4 (2026-06-29): DOWNGRADED Serbia (SRB) from visa_free (90 days) to visa_on_arrival (30 days, AUD $100 fee). The 2026-06-27 correction was premature — it elevated Serbia to visa_free based on Serbia MFA's statement 'a visa is not required', but this language reflects the universal 30-day VOA available to ALL nationalities, not a distinct bilateral waiver in force. Both Wikipedia (Visa policy of Tuvalu, updated 2026) and the srbija.gov.rs announcement confirm only that the agreement was SIGNED on 21 November 2024, not ratified. The bilateral agreement must enter into force before Serbia can be classified as visa_free. Classified as standard paid VOA (30 days, AUD $100) pending ratification and entry into force. Source: https://www.srbija.gov.rs/vest/en/237433/serbia-tuvalu-abolish-visas-for-holders-of-ordinary-passports.php + https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Visa_policy_of_Tuvalu

UNCHANGED (2026-06-29): All Schedule 1 VOA entries, EU waiver (visa_free), Taiwan (visa_free), UK (VOA Schedule 1 free), Ireland (paid VOA), CBI/RBI/fast_track — no new evidence requiring change. gov.tv still ECONNREFUSED; tuvaluhc.org.nz/visa still SSL expired.

outbound_official.note: no official Tuvalu government outbound visa-list page located; rely on destination-side data

sources_checked.description: Tuvalu Immigration Regulations 2014 - official PDF hosted on Tuvalu Permanent Mission to UN site. Contains Schedule 1 country list. Binary PDF - content not directly readable in this run.
Immigration Regulations CAP 24.15.1 - 2022 Revised Edition from official Tuvalu Legislation website. Schedule 1 identical to 2014 version. Binary PDF - content not directly readable in this run.

Tuvalu Permanent Mission to the UN - Consular Services page. Confirms entry permits for maximum one-month stay issued on arrival; no visa required for any nationality for stays up to 30 days. LOADED SUCCESSFULLY.

Full text of EU-Tuvalu Short-Stay Visa Waiver Agreement from EUR-Lex. Confirms Ireland AND UK excluded. 90 days/180-day period. Covers ordinary, diplomatic, service, official and special passport holders. LOADED SUCCESSFULLY.
EU External Action Service announcement of EU-Tuvalu visa waiver agreement signing (1 July 2016). Confirms 90 days/180-day period. Does not specify Ireland exclusion. LOADED SUCCESSFULLY.
Serbia Ministry of Foreign Affairs - Tuvalu travel page. Confirms 'a visa is not required for Serbian passport holders' visiting Tuvalu. Official government source. LOADED SUCCESSFULLY.
Serbian Government news announcement of Serbia-Tuvalu visa abolition agreement signed 21 November 2024. Confirms signing date and parties. Does not confirm ratification date. LOADED SUCCESSFULLY.

US State Department Tuvalu travel page. Confirms visitor permits on arrival, AUD $100 fee, 1-month validity, 6-month passport validity requirement. Does not list fee-exempt nationalities. LOADED SUCCESSFULLY.
Official Tuvalu Government immigration page - unreachable (ECONNREFUSED) during this validation.
Tuvalu High Commission visa page - expired SSL certificate, inaccessible.

Wikipedia Visa policy of Tuvalu - non-official. Used to corroborate Schedule 1 country list and identify Serbia's inclusion in 90-day visa-free list. Wikipedia article still describes Serbia agreement as 'yet to be ratified' - potentially outdated vs. Serbia MFA. LOADED SUCCESSFULLY.
Wikipedia Visa requirements for Serbian citizens - shows 'visa not required, 90 days within 180 days' for Tuvalu. Consistent with Serbia MFA. Non-official corroboration. LOADED SUCCESSFULLY.

Serbian Government news announcement of Serbia-Tuvalu visa abolition agreement signed 21 November 2024. Confirms SIGNED status only; no ratification date. Re-fetched 2026-06-29 to verify ratification status. LOADED SUCCESSFULLY.

Wikipedia Visa policy of Tuvalu - re-fetched 2026-06-29. Confirms Serbia-Tuvalu agreement 'yet to be ratified' as of 2026. Lists Schedule 1 countries for free VOA: 35 entries including American Samoa, Antigua and Barbuda, Bahamas, Belize, Cayman Islands, Cook Islands, Fiji, Gambia, Gibraltar, Grenada, Hong Kong, Jamaica, Kenya, Kiribati, Lesotho, Malawi, Malaysia, Maldives, Mauritius, Montserrat, Nauru, Niue, Northern Mariana Islands, Samoa, Saint Kitts and Nevis, Saint Lucia, Saint Vincent and the Grenadines, South Korea, Tanzania, Tonga, Trinidad and Tobago, Uganda, United Kingdom, Vanuatu, Zambia. LOADED SUCCESSFULLY.
